pages.full_text:
Silvacane +
Sénanque soon
followed. Le
Thoronet had
20 monks +
20 lay bros.
(from Magon in
the Ardèche
originally.)
I've never seen
such a plain
building as
this one. This
is one of the
very few
ornaments.
We saw
an amazing trick sculpture with a candle which
only had a flame when you looked through the
box. (Mirrors.) It's quite similar to Sénanques.
Lunch there then on to La Garde Freinet; a
lovely Mediaeval Village. V. Good wine shop
Le Cellier run by Hervé Sauvage. I bought
a dress from a shop ~~one~~ owned by an
English woman. Then we came down down
down the hills to [?] Plan de la Tour.
pages.notes: Page contains a large photo of Le Thoronet abbey interior with two smaller circular photos. The right-side text continues from the previous spread. Some text on the far right margin is cut off at the gutter. 'Magon' could possibly read 'Maçon' but 'Magon' appears more likely given context (Ardèche). The strikethrough word 'one' in 'a shop one owned' is a correction. No handwritten page number visible in top-left corner.

pages.full_text:
A lovely campsite [?Thoraine] in an open field with
vines, a stream, an old damn. An old woman
rang the camp owners for us. Just after
she drove off, we heard a car crash +
Simon realised that she'd driven out too
slowly + another car had hit her car.
Nice fascilities in an old ~~burnt~~ barn.

There's a hunting dog + I've seen 2 wild
boar the other side of the stream.
The boars trot along with their tails straight up
in the air.
__Thursday June 20th.__
We set off from this lovely, quiet campsite where we
camped alone + soon joined a ghastly queue going into
St. Tropez. Eventually we reached __Domaine du Rayol__,
a Mediterranean garden which has collected plants from
hot places all over the world.
                    NZ Eucalyptus.
pages.notes: Page number 28 visible top-right. The campsite name at the start of the page is partially cut off — reads '[?Thoraine]' or similar; the top of the R image shows the tail end of the campsite name which is unclear. 'fascilities' is the diary's spelling (sic). The strikethrough word appears to be 'burnt' corrected to 'barn'. 'Thursday June 20th' falls on a Thursday in 2002 and 2013; given the South of France / St. Tropez context and volume B covering 2003-2019, a Thursday June 20th could be 2013 — confidence is low without more anchoring evidence. Two photos at the bottom with caption 'view of sea from cafe' and a second unlabelled photo.
